foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Preoperative assessment evaluates the patient's metabolic and physiological reserve; understanding homeostatic mechanisms helps identify patients at risk for decompensation under surgical stress

foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Informed consent is a mandatory ethical and legal prerequisite before any surgical procedure; understanding autonomy, beneficence, and capacity assessment is integral to preoperative evaluation

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Preoperative assessment findings directly dictate the perioperative plan including monitoring level, fluid strategy, and anesthesia choice

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Preoperative hemoglobin assessment and blood typing/crossmatching determine transfusion readiness for anticipated surgical blood loss

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Preoperative workup relies on interpreting baseline blood tests (CBC, RFT, LFT, coagulation), ECG, chest X-ray, and risk-specific imaging to stratify surgical risk

objectives: SU11.1: Describe principles of Preoperative assessment.
SU11.2: Enumerate the principles of general, regional, and local
SU11.3: Demonstrate maintenance of an airway in a mannequin or
SU11.4: Enumerate the indications and principles of day care General
SU11.5: Describe principles of providing post-operative pain relief and
SU11.6: Describe Principles of safe General Surgery
integration: This undergraduate-level roadmap connects Preoperative Assessment across foundational sciences and clinical applications.
